pi_items.source: task.json description and setup_task.sh scripts both reference this path
Mathematical calculation: base plate (120×80×5=48000 mm³) minus total hole volumes (4×π×(2.1)²×5 + π×(4.2)²×5 = 176.4π ≈ 554.177 mm³) = 47445.823 mm³
Task specification for acceptable tolerance; used in verifier.py line 35 for volume accuracy check
Task specification for acceptable dimension tolerance per axis; used in verifier.py line 79 for bbox comparison
Task requires: 4 M4 mounting holes + 1 M8 center hole = 5 total holes. Each hole creates a cylindrical face when subtracted from the solid.

privileged_info_summary: Task creates a synthetic motor mounting plate in FreeCAD with verified dimensions of 120×80×5 mm. The target volume is 47445.8 mm³ (base plate 48000 mm³ minus 5 holes totaling 554.177 mm³). The part has 4 M4 mounting holes at corners (8,8), (8,72), (112,8), (112,72) and 1 centered M8 hole at (60,40). All specifications are mathematically consistent: the calculated net volume matches the claimed target volume within rounding, bbox dimensions match the base plate size, and the minimum 5 cylindrical faces corresponds exactly to the 5 required holes.
